elements.sent-2411-003: We characterize a class of superclassical non-Markovian open quantum system dynamics that are defined by their lack of measurement invasiveness when the corresponding observable commutates with the pre-measurement state.
elements.sent-2411-004: This diagonal non-invasiveness guarantees that joint probabilities for measurement outcomes fulfill classical Kolmogorov consistency conditions.
elements.sent-2411-005: These features are fulfilled regardless of the previous (measurement) system history and are valid at arbitrary later times after an arbitrary system initialization.
elements.sent-2411-006: It is shown that a subclass of depolarizing dynamics, which are based on a (time-irreversible) non-unitary system-environment coupling, satisfy the required properties.
elements.sent-2411-007: The relationship with other operational [Milz et al., Phys.. Rev.
elements.sent-2411-008: X 10, 041049 (2020)] and non-operational [Banacki et al., Phys.. Rev.
elements.sent-2411-009: A 107, 032202 (2023)] notions of classicality in non-Markovian open quantum systems is studied in detail and exemplified through different examples.
elements.sent-2411-010: The notion of memory, or equivalently non-Markovianity, in open quantum systems~\cite{breuerbook,vega}\ has been tackled from different perspectives.
elements.sent-2411-011: Broadly speaking, the contributions of the last decades can be split in two different lines of research.
elements.sent-2411-012: In non-operational approaches, the presence of memory is determined by taking into account departures of the system density matrix propagator with respect to a Lindblad equation~\cite{BreuerReview,plenioReview .}
elements.sent-2411-013: On the contrary, in operational approaches the system is subjected to a series of consecutive measurements.
elements.sent-2411-014: The corresponding outcome probabilities determine the Markovianity or non-Markovianity of the open system dynamics~\cite {modiNM,CPF .}
elements.sent-2411-015: Based on these complementary approaches, different definitions of classicality of open quantum dynamics has been recently proposed in the literature~\cite {horoNoMark,Markol,garcia,maria,plenioExper,plenioNoMarkov,taranto .}
elements.sent-2411-016: The notion of environment-to-system backflow of information~\cite {BreuerReview is a cornerstone in the definition of memory effects in non-operational approaches.}
elements.sent-2411-017: In Ref.~\cite{horoNoMark the authors proposed a criterion to distinguish classical from quantum information backflows.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Essentially, this non-Markovian effect is classified as classical if the system propagator can be written as a composition of a (classical) probability map followed by a system unitary transformation.
elements.sent-2411-019: This (non-operational) definition of classicality makes sense because in such a case the role of the environment can be mimicked without involving any quantum feature.
elements.sent-2411-020: In operational approaches~\cite {Markol,garcia,maria,plenioExper,plenioNoMarkov,taranto classicality has been tackled from a different perspective.}
elements.sent-2411-021: Given a fixed measurement basis, the system is classical (behaves as a classical one) if Kolmogorov consistency conditions are satisfied, that is, the joint probability for an arbitrary subset of measurement processes can be obtained by marginalization of the joint probability of the full measurement set~\cite{breuerbook .}
elements.sent-2411-022: Equivalently, the dynamics (strictly the outcome statistics) is classical if (in the chosen basis) a non-selective measurement~\cite{breuerbook performed at an arbitrary time is noninvasive, that is, it does not modify the subsequent measurement statistics.}
elements.sent-2411-023: Classicality in operational approaches has been analyzed in both Markovian and non-Markovian regimes.
elements.sent-2411-024: In the Markovian case~\cite{Markol,garcia , the outcome statistics is classical (Kolmogorov conditions are fulfilled) when, in the chosen measurement basis, the system does not develop any quantum coherence.}
elements.sent-2411-025: In the non-Markovian regime, the absence of coherences in the measurement basis does not guaranty classicality.
elements.sent-2411-026: In fact, in this case the role of the coherences is played instead by the system-environment ($s$-$e$) quantum discord~\cite{plenioNoMarkov .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Furthermore, even when the dynamics induces coherent behaviors, or quantum discord, the measurement statistics can appear as a classical one when the developed quantum features cannot be detected in the chosen measurement basis (non-coherence-generating-and detecting maps~\cite{Markol and non-discord-generating-and detecting maps~ \cite{plenioNoMarkov respectively).}}
elements.sent-2411-028: In a previous paper~\cite{DNI , based on an operational scheme, a deep relationship between measurement invasiveness and the presence of memory effects has been established.}
elements.sent-2411-029: The operational scheme consists on performing a set of three consecutive (projective) measurement processes.
elements.sent-2411-030: The first and last are arbitrary, while the intermediate one is performed in the basis where the pre-measurement system state is diagonal.
elements.sent-2411-031: This last constraint eliminates the possibility of any measurement invasiveness in the Markovian (operationally defined) case.
elements.sent-2411-032: Therefore, violation of diagonal non-invasiveness (DNI) becomes an indicator of non-classical (quantum) memory effects.
elements.sent-2411-033: This univocal relationship was established for open quantum systems whose dynamics is derived from a full unitary microscopic description or from stochastic Hamiltonian models.
elements.sent-2411-034: However, when the underlying $s$-$e$ couplings is non-unitary (bipartite Lindblad equations), even when the previous and posterior measurement processes are arbitrary, DNI could be fulfilled in presence of memory effects.
elements.sent-2411-035: The corresponding system dynamics are termed as superclassical.
elements.sent-2411-036: The main goal of this contribution is to characterize superclassical non-Markovian open quantum dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-037: Of particular interest is to compare this notion of classicality with previous non-operational~\cite{horoNoMark and operational approaches~\cite{plenioNoMarkov .}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Furthermore, we ask about which kind of system dynamics could fulfill the corresponding definitions.
elements.sent-2411-039: The answer to this question involves the family of depolarizing dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-040: Interestingly, these dynamics play a central role not only in this quantum-classical border, but also in the modeling of noise sources in quantum information platforms~\cite {HoroEntangled,bose,amosov,cirac,mancini,faniza,learn,chau,self,he and related theoretical and experimental problems~\cite {sasaki,narang,kim,sudarshan,mexico,cresser,petruccione,banerje .}}

elements.section-2411-002: Definition of classicality in presence of memory effects
elements.sent-2411-047: In this section we review and compare the different notion of classicality when considering non-Markovian open quantum systems.
elements.sent-2411-048: In the approach of Ref.~\cite{horoNoMark , an information backflow (non-Markovian memory effect) is considered classical if the system propagator $\Lambda _{t,0}$ assumes the structure \begin{equation}\rho _{t}=\Lambda _{t,0}[\rho _{0}]=U_{t}\Lambda _{t,0}^{\mathrm{cl}}[\rho _{0}]U_{t}^{\dag }, \label{JoroClassPropa}\end{equation} where $\rho _{0}$\ is the system initial condition.}
elements.sent-2411-049: Furthermore, $U_{t}$ is an arbitrary (system) unitary transformation while \begin{equation}\Lambda _{t,0}^{\mathrm{cl}}[\rho _{0}]=\sum_{c,c^{\prime }}\Lambda _{cc^{\prime }}(t)|c\rangle \langle c^{\prime }|\rho _{0}|c^{\prime }\rangle \langle c|. \label{ProbaMap}\end{equation} Here, the coefficients $\{\Lambda _{cc^{\prime (t)\}$ define a map (propagator) in the space of classical probability vectors $\{p_{c}\},$ that is, $p_{c (t)=\sum_{c^{\prime }\Lambda _{cc^{\prime (t)p_{c^{\prime (0).}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-050: Classicality is also valid when the propagator can be written as a time-independent convex combination \begin{equation}\Lambda _{t,0}=\sum_{i}p_{i}\Lambda _{t,0}^{(i)}[\rho _{0}], \label{convex}\end{equation} where each map $\Lambda _{t,0 ^{(i)}$ fulfills Eq.~(\ref{JoroClassPropa ).}}
elements.sent-2411-051: In the approach of Ref.~\cite{plenioNoMarkov , the definition of classicality involves the bipartite $s$-$e$ propagator, $\mathcal{G _{t+\tau ,t .$ Furthermore, it is intrinsically related to a\ chosen complete basis $\{|c\rangle \}$ of system states.}}}
elements.sent-2411-052: The dynamics is classical if \begin{equation}\bigtriangleup \circ \mathcal{G}_{t+\tau ,t}\circ \bigtriangleup \circ \mathcal{G}_{t,0}\circ \bigtriangleup =\bigtriangleup \circ \mathcal{G} _{t+\tau ,t}\circ \mathcal{G}_{t,0}\circ \bigtriangleup , \label{Fixed}\end{equation} where $\circ $\ denotes composition and $\bigtriangleup \lbrack \bullet ]=\sum_{c |c\rangle \langle c|\bullet |c\rangle \langle c|$ is a complete dephasing map in the chosen system basis.}
elements.sent-2411-053: Reading this map as the action of a non-selective measurement, the equality~(\ref{Fixed ) guaranty the fulfillment of (classical) Kolmogorov conditions of the corresponding outcome probabilities~\cite{plenioNoMarkov .}}
elements.sent-2411-054: For simplicity, in the above brief overview we have taken the initial time $t_{0 =0$ and two posterior arbitrary times $t$ and $t+\tau .$ Assuming a initial bipartite state with null discord $\rho _{0 ^{se =\sum_{c |c\rangle \langle c|\otimes \sigma _{c ,$ where $\{\sigma _{c}\}$ are environment states, the above definition can be fulfilled when the bipartite propagator do not create quantum discord in the chosen system basis.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-055: Alternatively, the propagator can create quantum discord but this discord cannot be detected by means of later measurement processes performed on the system.
elements.sent-2411-056: These two cases define the non-discord-generating-and-detecting maps~\cite{plenioNoMarkov .}
elements.sent-2411-057: The operational scheme of Ref.~\cite{DNI establishes a connection between measurement invasiveness and \textquotedblleft non-classical\textquotedblright\ memory effects.}
elements.sent-2411-058: The main ingredient is the DNI property of Markovian dynamics, that is, these dynamics are unaffected by a projective measurement whose observable commutates with the pre-measurement state.
elements.sent-2411-059: This property is independent of the previous system history.
elements.sent-2411-060: Non-Markovian dynamics that fulfill DNI for arbitrary (previous and \textquotedblleft posterior\textquotedblright) measurement histories are termed as superclassical.
elements.sent-2411-061: In terms of the bipartite propagator $ \mathcal{G _{t+\tau ,t}$ they are defined by the equality \begin{equation}\bigtriangleup _{Z}\circ \mathcal{G}_{t+\tau ,t}\circ \bigtriangleup _{Y}^{t}\circ \mathcal{G}_{t,0}\circ \bigtriangleup _{X}=\bigtriangleup _{Z}\circ \mathcal{G}_{t+\tau ,t}\circ \mathcal{G}_{t,0}\circ \bigtriangleup _{X}. \label{DeltaSuper}\end{equation} In contrast to Eq.~(\ref{Fixed ), this equality must be valid for arbitrary dephasing maps $\bigtriangleup _{X}$ and $\bigtriangleup _{Z ,$ which correspond to arbitrary measurement processes, $\bigtriangleup _{M [\bullet ]=\sum_{m |m\rangle \langle m|\bullet |m\rangle \langle m|,$ where $m=x,y,z.$ Furthermore, $\bigtriangleup _{Y ^{t}$ is the measurement dephasing map that leaves invariant the pre-measurement system state $\rho _{t|X ,$ where \begin{equation}\rho _{t|X}\equiv \mathrm{Tr}_{e}(\mathcal{G}_{t,0}[\bigtriangleup _{X}[\rho _{0}]\otimes \sigma _{0}])=\sum_{c}p_{t}^{c}|c_{t}\rangle \langle c_{t}|. \label{EigenSystem}\end{equation} The bipartite initial condition is $\rho _{0 ^{se =\rho _{0}\otimes \sigma _{0 .$ The expression for $\rho _{t|X}$ implies that $\bigtriangleup _{Y ^{t $ must to commutates with the eigenbasis $\{|c_{t}\rangle \}.$ Equivalently, \begin{equation}\bigtriangleup _{Y}^{t}[\rho _{t|X}]=\rho _{t|X}. \label{YtMeaurement}\end{equation} Notice that the basis $\{|c_{t}\rangle \},$ as well as the eigenvalues $ \{p_{t ^{c}\},$ are in general time-dependent.}}}}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-235: Broadly speaking, the contributions of the last decades can be split in two different lines of research~\cite{notapie .}
